raw_text: Bangalore’s roads have become nothing short of a deathtrap. Specifically, the stretch from Silk Board towards Eco Space is absolutely horrendous! The service roads are filled with potholes so big, they could fit a 1BHK flat! And I’m not exaggerating. Stones and debris are scattered everywhere, making it downright life-threatening to even drive or ride through these roads. One wrong move, and it could be fatal. And the worst part? This is happening in a city where we pay some of the highest RTO charges in the country! Where does all that money even go? Certainly not into fixing our roads. It’s infuriating to see that despite paying through the nose for vehicle registration, basic infrastructure is non-existent. Traffic here is already unbearable. It takes hours just to cover 5-6 kilometers. And to top it all off, when they finally do attempt to “fix” the roads, they just dump loose gravel into the potholes. There’s no actual repair work, no real effort to solve the problem. It’s clear the authorities know about this mess, but they just don’t care. This is all a direct result of corruption in the BBMP and the utter negligence of our government. It’s mind-boggling how things can be this bad, and no one is held accountable. At this point, it’s more than just frustrating – it’s saddening. Every day, people struggle on these roads, just trying to go about their daily lives. Watching this unfold is not only infuriating, but it also makes you wonder if anything will ever change in this city. Is it too much to ask for basic infrastructure after paying so much in taxes and fees? We deserve better.
clean_text: bangalore roads described as deathtrap especially service roads from silk board to ecospace filled with huge potholes stones and debris poor temporary repairs and corruption suspected
summary: Detailed rant about extremely hazardous service roads on Silk Board–Ecospace stretch, poor repairs, traffic congestion and alleged corruption.

raw_text: Recently moved to bangalore and apart from the traffic and the exorbitant rents, poor garbage collection system has stood out as the city's highlight. Not to add if you're living away from the roadside there's nothing you can do except going and checking downstairs every time there's a truck sound. The thing that angers me is that even when the system/government/politics work people refuse to help in the betterment of the city/country. Then simply complain that everyone is corrupt & that Bangalore is not a good place to live. I get that corruption occurs on a massive scale but why not help in what small things do get done. The bins are fully empty inside as you can see, dry & wet waste can be easily separated & can be put in respective bins but people simply refuse to use them properly, they simply leave waste(unseparated) on the footpath & BBMP workers have to separate the waste by hand, I have talked to these workers & they can't really do anything to change people. PS: I have seen people who dump waste here it is not the regular family, it is the richer people. They either drop waste while going to their jobs in their ''Big'' cars or while on walk-in shoes that cost more than the rent of a 1bhk house.

raw_text: Hi, i am dr.srirama narayana rao, residing at no.881, 1st A main, kengeri satellite town, bangalore. the sewege draining main pipes run in 6th d cross and 1st A main, are the main drainage lines which were laid during the development of the locality that is, in 1970's. now the load on drainage is too much and the main road ,and main area which has grown on the other side also drain to the same line. the pipes laid are earthern at that time of 1970 and they are not bothered to change the direction of flow which has to drain straight and to low direction. these line takes so many bends and sufficient no manholes also. adjacent 1st main road has become main arterial RING ROAD and all commercial waste is thrown into this line which gets blocked every day and sewege overflows onto the road giving foul smell, not able to walk on the street and terrific mosquito breeding causing all the health hazards. it is since good no. of years we have requested the concerned authorities but all in vain.so many big trees has randomly grown and their roots have wrecked the pipes also. there are good no.of illegal running of hotels and bars who dump all non degradable and solid waste into the same drains further blocking the drainage. i request whether your forum can help us in, 1. change the pipes line to more wider and straighter. 2.divert the main flow to more efficient pipelines, 3. bring the commercial establishments who are violating the rules, to legal books, 4. in association with other govt departments like forest try to trim the roots affecting the flow, 5.bring health dept to notice the hazardous out flow of sewege , main reason for already spread dengue, malaria and other communicable diseases we are saying so many deaths reported. hope change will occur soon. thanking you.

That is why these problems keep recurring. Is it so hard for BWSSB to ensure that problems are fixed properly in the first instance?

raw_text: BWSSB has dug up huge trenches in the Main streets around Laggere Main Road and most other approach roads to Laggere, Peenya 3rd Phase, Bangalore. The area which spans around 4 square kms and houses atleast 75000 to 1 lakh people now has no connectivity since the buses are not able to ply the streets and the cars cannot be taken out of the garages. Huge trenches have been dug out both in the Main streets and in every side road, right in front of each and every house. The work has been going on from more than 3 months and has already taken 1 person's life and has left several people injured. The dust and dirt caused by this activity is unbearable and is causing several health problems to the residents. The work is simply taking atleast 10 times more time than what is actually needed. It is a shame on the part of BWSSB and BBMP for such lousy planning and execution of such a huge and important project. It is high time for the concerned officials to wake up and respond to the concerns of the residents.

Please advise on what has to be done in this regard.

raw_text: This is at M.M.Layout, Kavalbyrasandra, R.T.Nagar, Bangalore. We placed a request with the BWSSB that the drainage near our house was blocked. They called us up during weekdays when we were not at home, and they deliberately did not come to clear the drain because if they came, there would be nobody to give them their bribe. We gave them the exact address and even our streets are numbered, but they gave an excuse that we did not specify the address correctly. So then they came on Saturday with their drainage clearing truck, did the job and said 'kalsi saar'. Which in Kannada means 'send us off sir'. It's their way of saying 'Give us our bribe and we will be on our way'. They expect more than Rs.200 for a task that should be done for free. Could you please ensure that these people just come and do their jobs even if it's on a weekday when we're not at home? And also to ensure that they don't ask for a bribe. The corporator is one person who can ensure this.

I along with my other neighbours of my street have lodged several complaints with RT Nagar BWSSB office and i have even registered central complaint. Interestingly water is being left in adjacent streets in sufficient quantities. Why discriminate only our street. we are now forced to purchase water at exorbitant rates from private water tankers. May i request the concerned authorities to look into our problem and set it right with immediate effect.

raw_text: So it's around 9 PM in the night. I come to Jayanagar 1st Block to drop my friend and her sister off at her place. I park the car right opposite her house (there's a Hopcoms right in front). We're sitting there in the car saying our goodbyes and half a minute later a Police Patrol Car arrives. They see us and go past. The next thing I know they come back in reverse and ask me to step out. I open my car door and ask them if there has been any problem. The cop on the driver's seat says 'Nimgoskara ne bandvi' (translates to we came here for you). I'm puzzled at this point and I ask him 'Why for us?'. He tells me that he's received a complaint for someone causing a brawl after drinking and shows an old complaint on his tablet or (whatever they use). To this I reply saying 'Sir we just arrived here. I came here to drop off my friends.' He doesn't believe me and says that he's taking us to the station. I insisted him to use a breath analyser and check me. I question him asking how 2 girls and a guy can cause a brawl, to which he replies rudely saying 'Nim yogyate ella gothu' (roughly translates to we know what you guys are) I politely ask him not to accuse us of something that we did not do. At this point he wants to take down all of our names and addresses. I informed him that my friend and her sister live in this building and he is free to check with her parents and neighbours. I also give him my DL and ID proof. Now as the other officer is taking down my name he accuses me of drinking in the car and says he will take me to the police station, to which I told him that he is free to check my car. I also tell him that he can keep my ID proof and take down my phone number. By this time he sees my friend's neighbours coming down and says 'Illa bidu mask case haaki mugsu' (Leave it, put a case for not wearing a mask) Unfortunately, one of us had removed our mask as we reached our destination. They take 400 from us and then say 'Illa bidi olleavre neevu' (it's ok you are good people) and leave by the time her neighbours come there. While leaving he also instructed us not to sit in the car 'Ee thara car alli kuthkolodella madbedi' (Don't sit in the car like this) To add to this, they didn't give me a bill/receipt and made me keep the 400₹ on the dashboard and didn't touch it when I was handing them the money for the 'fine'. Many of us have had experiences like this in the past few months. It's a bit concerning. Did we do something wrong here? We were seated in a car in front of my friend's house in a prime residential area. It wasn't even that late in the night. Doesn't this count to harassment of the public? Open to critisism.

We are not defaulters or fugitives, and if the rwa doesn't like feedback or the way we express feedback, they cannot take away services we pay for. The president is not scared of anyone, because we told him what he's doing is illegal, but no use. We don't think police or legal will be of help here. What can be done? We apologized too, 4 times, he keeps on finding faults with that and pushing the reinstatment of services to some later day. This is pure dictatorship. They treat us tenants like second class ppl. Is this a common practice in bangalore where rwa has the rights to block a rent and maintenance paying, law abiding citizen from accessing paid services?

raw_text: What is up with the men here? I put on a little makeup, do my hair and dress up to go to a restaurant on Magrath road and people (mostly men) look at women like they have just seen some kind of weird alien who has descended on earth. What is up with the continuous staring? What is there to stare at for so long? From the auto drivers who stop randomly without me even raising even so much as a finger and just stare for a good five minutes to hooligans who have actually catcalled and sexually harassed me, this city feels so effing unsafe everytime I step out. I carry a pepper spray with me almost all the time because I'm scared and I never know when I may have to use it. All my senses are on alert in order to protect myself. It disappoints me to the core that a woman can't just go out to enjoy dinner/lunch without feeling unsafe.

raw_text: This is my first time posting on Reddit. I have always just been a reader and never commented before. After today I cannot hold it in anymore. I am exhausted and I do not have the energy to keep brushing it off. I am a 20F living in Bengaluru. Today around 7 pm I was at a small provision store near my house. I was wearing a loose trouser, a loose shirt, and a black scarf covering my hair Two guys on a scooter passed by and catcalled me. The one sitting behind looked right at me with a big smug smile, like harassing me was some kind of achievement. I did not have the strength to shout back or react. I just stood there staring, too drained to do anything. I could not even process what disgusting words they said. It does not matter what a woman wears. She will still be harassed. I was 9 years old the first time it happened. A teenage boy groped me and ran. I have been followed while wearing my school uniform. I have been followed to the bus stop while fully covered. I have been touched, commented on, laughed at, and made to feel unsafe more times than I can count. Just a few days ago I was getting on a bus. A boy, maybe in 11th or 12th grade, deliberately pushed himself against me. When I looked at him he moved away and laughed with his friends. His female friend asked him in Tamil, “You did it knowingly, right?” and then laughed along with him. That disgusted me even more. Seeing another woman normalize it made it worse. It is not just strangers either. Have received unsolicited d*ck pictures from my father’s side of the family. How much are women expected to tolerate? Most of the time I fight back. I shout, glare, or try to scare them off. But there are also times like today when I just freeze. I doubt myself. I wonder if I am overreacting. But there are too many incidents now for it to be in my head. I live in Bengaluru, which is supposed to be a “safe city.” What kind of safety is this?

raw_text: I (23F)was driving home with my family in my dad’s car, in the infamous Bangalore traffic which only gets worse when it rains. We were stuck for over an hour near K R puram. While waiting, I was glancing around, and that’s when I made brief eye contact with a man in another car he seemed to be with his family too. To my shock and disgust, he had the audacity to make a kissing gesture at me. I was so taken aback that I immediately looked away. I wish I’d had the presence of mind to call him out or even just flip him off, but with my family around, I just couldn’t bring myself to react. As if that wasn’t enough, later that same night, a random delivery guy had the nerve to wink at me. Two completely unsolicited, disgusting encounters in the span of just a few hours. It’s honestly infuriating how some men have absolutely no sense of respect or decency. No matter the setting public spaces, heavy traffic, even when they’re with their own families they behave shamelessly. It’s exhausting and gross.

raw_text: I am new to Bangalore. Last night, around 11:45 p.m., I was walking back to my place from my brother's, something I've done many times before. I had my laptop, a backpack, and realized too late that I’d forgotten my glasses. As I exited his building, I noticed two middle-aged men on a bike drive past, only to stop about 50 meters ahead near a tea point, acting as though they were taking pictures. Their behavior seemed odd, so I pretended to be on a call and walked past them. One of the men suddenly asked, 'Ma'am, where do you live?' Startled, I responded with, 'Why do you care?' and quickly turned back, heading towards my brother's building as fast as I could. They kept repeating the question, following me as I walked back. I made it inside and rushed up to my brother's floor, with them watching until they saw me enter. Even then, they lingered outside, waiting. Once I was safely upstairs, I called my brother, telling him someone had followed me. That seemed to scare them off, and they left. My brother came down immediately and returned about 10 minutes later, this time accompanied by two police officers on a different bike. One of the officers was smiling as I recounted what happened. Then, in broken English, they explained that it was them the whole time and that they were “patrolling” to prevent theft in the area. They hadn’t been in uniform initially and were on a completely different bike, which added to the confusion. I was shaken to the core by the incident. I couldn't help but question why they hadn’t identified themselves as police officers and why they waited until I called my brother before leaving. Why would they ask me where I lived without clarifying who they were? I'm left wondering if this is common or if anyone else in the area has had a similar experience. Is this really how patrolling works here?

raw_text: My friend (23F) and I (23M) were going to a Ganeshotsava and walking in the student neighbourhood of SG Palya just beside Christ University. She was walking just beside the footpath on the road, and I was on the footpath. We were hurrying to catch an auto a few metres away when this Rickshaw with 3 (local) guys in it drove the auto harmfully close to her to take a cut and hooted at her. She just jumped on the footpath as a knee-jerk reaction, and I was frozen then and there. She continued her walk, and I just looked back to find one of the guys staring at me when the auto went a bit ahead and slowed down. I could not do anything. I felt so guilty that I cried out to both of my friends, and their reaction shocked me. They did not think it was a big deal. My friend continued her rant about me getting late rather than this thing just happening to her. I was numbed the entire evening. They tried explaining to me that these instances are pretty common and have happened with them a lot of times, but the fact that it happened in front of me, with a close friend of mine, and I just was unresponsive is eating me from inside. I did not note the vehicle number; all I remember is the face of one of those harassers, which I can never forget. I could have protested, raised my voice or done SOMETHING about it, but I did not, and the only motive to put it on here is to alert all the Women out there constantly to stay safe and to all the Men out there to not regret like me later. It's been a year since I moved here, and until yesterday, I had a somewhat favourable opinion on the issue of the safety of women in this city; yesterday, it just shattered any such notions. I do not consider my company safe enough for any of my female friends anymore even though they expressed that they still do. Has anyone faced something similar to this before? I need to know how you cope with something like this. Happened on Saturday that went by. At around 1 pm As you know the traffic in that area. I was following a lane and was sticking to it. In comes a dude clad in white kurta asking me to move. He came in from the wrong direction. He kept on shouting and I did move as there were vehicles in front of me and on my right side too. He wanted me to reverse, so that he could go. He first hit my car. I went out to see the damage and I asked him. He started calling my partner chutiya. My partner called him neene chutiya A group of 6 kurta clad men came and assaulted both of us. The police came in after 20 mins and asked us to first leave the place as it was a riot prone area. There were only 2 police men. Constables We did nothing to aggravate yet we are meted out with this. We escaped the situation as we knew that this was a provocation and we did not want to approach the police as we had a child amidst us

raw_text: This literally happened to me literally just a couple hours ago(around 12am). I(20M. Bangalore resident for 4 years)had just got back from my native(Kerala). I got down at Silk Board Jn. I usually take an Uber but today I don't know why I was absent minded. A big dark dude came up to me and said he'll drop me at my location. I said it's fine I had already booked(I hadn't and he could see it). He said he won't charge extra and they have this government app that will show them the right fare. I was like screw it whatever and just got in his auto. I told him the landmark of where I wanted to go(Mahadevapura area). He said it's fine. 'Mahadevapura only right? I know it.' I got a bit doubtful with his reaction for some reason. We started off. I'm not fluent in Kannada and barely know a few words. So we spoke in Tamil. He understood and asked me what my language was. I told him and he started loudly playing some music of said language. I let my gaurd down a bit after this and thought 'maybe this dude is chill after all.' A while later, conveniently somewhere near a not-so-lit area on the road, his auto started racing and he stopped the auto. He said some gear valve issue is there and his friend will be here soon with the wire or something. Alarm bells we're going off in my head at this point. He called someone and said he's standing in this particular area and bring the wire and come. I immediately started booking an Uber auto sitting in his vehicle. Now a small detail about me is that I'm 5'2ish and look like a bit like a kid. And I was carrying a heavy backpack and a suitcase.(I'm also very introverted and want to avoid conflict/confrontation as much as possible).So I was in a very compromising position all things considered. I got out and told him that I had booked another ride and that it was urgent and I had to go home. I said I'll pay him ₹50 for bringing me till here atleast(less than 3 kilometres). He said it's gonna be 100 and wouldn't take no for an answer. I was slowly starting to panic at this point so I said ok fine. I pulled out my gpay and to my luck my network was down. I pulled out my wallet and gave him a brand new ₹100 note. I turned around to pick up my suitcase and I heard a rip like paper tearing. I immediately looked back and he said this note is torn and that he won't take it. I told him then that I heard him tear the note and that I'm not an idiot. He said 'you gave me the note like this'.He started raising his voice slightly. By this time my auto has reached and is waiting for me(bless that man). I was getting very frustrated and scared now. He gave me back my note which clearly looked like it had been torn just then. I asked him if he had change for ₹200. He said he did. He pulled the note out. I showed him my ₹200 note and said this one doesn't have any tear. Take it and leave me alone. His friend has been on call with him the whole time. Just when I picked up my suitcase and started walking to my Uber, another auto pulled up with a few people in it. All local looking men. This auto guy started talking to him and said 'he started booking in the auto itself and it's here. He's leaving now.' I got in the Uber and my auto driver asked me what was happening. I just told him to gun it, that it was a dangerous situation and that I'd tell him on the way. I told him what had happened in a panic which made mix Kannada,Tamil and Hindi in a broken jumbled mess. He told me to calm down and speak in Hindi. So I did. My phone's battery was at 3% which had added to my stress. We went ahead and we were travelling through Outer Ring Road. So the traffic at night due to the metro construction was insane. I calmed down finally and thought it's all over. Another detail is that my parents are not in Bengaluru at the moment. They are out of station. So I'm completely alone. I let one of my friends know about the situation and sent him my live location. I then notice an auto pull up on my right. To my horrific realisation, it's the friend of the first auto driver. Him and his passenger we're both glaring at me. At this point I'm freaked out. Coz now I understood that they're following me home. My auto guy went ahead and I told him that those guys are friends of the other auto guy. He told me understood and that's why he was speeding and gapping between vehicles with what limited space he had. And he did. I've never seen someone maneuver like this in my life. Man did everything in his power to lose the other auto and he somehow did.(I still kept looking back to see if they were following us all the way till I reached home). I finally reached home and my gpay was finally working. I paid him an extra ₹50 and thanked him a lot for helping me and now I'm sitting in my toilet taking a shit and typing this out peacefully. But I shudder to think what would've happened if didn't have the sense to book an Uber at the first sign of trouble. What would've happened if that other auto guy reached before my Uber?

raw_text: Guy on a bike tried to snatch phone on 27th Main HSR Layout.So last Friday (22/07), I was returning home on a yulu past 1am. While on the yulu I got a call and took my iPhone out to check who it was. Within a fraction of a minute what transpired next baffled me. 3 guys on a pulsar motorbike wearing masks sneaked up on me from the left side, close to the hand by which I was holding my phone, the guy sitting at the end tried to snatch my phone, but luckily I had a firm grip on it and he failed. They then sped up into the dark, before I could come to my senses and realize that I have been attempted to be robbed and note down the bike number. I used to consider HSR a safe locality to stay in and wander about in late at night but I have been proven wrong. There was a police car just a few hundred metres away.

raw_text: Recently, I was traveling from Majestic to Attibele on a BMTC bus. I sat on a seat beside a middle aged uncle who sat near window and the bus was not much crowded. The uncle beside me was watching through the window and i was distracted on my phone. There were few people standing in the middle of the bus. Everything was going smoothly, until one guy who was standing suddenly started coming towards me as if he was about to vomit. Immediately, I kept my phone in my pocket and left the seat to avoid him, thinking he was gonna vomit. He was also accompanied another guy who was holding him by the shoulder as he was like sick or drunken. The uncle who was sitting on the window seat couldn't come out as these two guys were locked him by covering the seats while one of the guy was coughing and about to vomit. The uncle was asking them to move so he could cough or vomit over the bus window but they didn't budge and coughed hard as if he was having some emergency. In the meantime, While intensely watching these two guys and uncle asking them to move, I felt the place got congested and decided to move towards the front of the bus where it was empty. While i turned my body to walk towards the bus front, I felt a strange feeling inside my left pant pocket as if some mice has entered inside. I was bewildered and pressed my hand against my pocket to see what it was. And it was the fingers of a guy standing beside (right side) me. He immediately took of his hands and I shouted while shocked at the same time 'Yaake pocketalli Kai aakthathiye?' I could not process what was happening completely but there was robbery attempt and i was checking all pockets if i lost anything. When i shouted at him, he kept quiet but the other guy was answering that he did not kept his hands and giving furious look towards me. While all these going on, bus stopped at a stop, in a moment all the guys flew away and the bus started moving. The above incident happened in a span of 3-4mins. Then i sat down and wondered what the hell happened. I was able to connect the dots that these 4-5 guys were altogether and two of them created a panic situation (vomiting) to create a distraction while other guys were executing a robbery. All of these happened so fast and i had no proof that one of them was attempting to rob me. Even the people in the bus were confused and able to understand once all of those guys left the immediately when i confronted one of them. I was lucky that day otherwise i would've been a victim and beaten myself for being careless and got robbed. More than that, I was shocked to see how much the robbers have teamed up, planned and tried to execute a robbery in a running bus during the daytime, while surrounded by a number of people. I have huge respect for this city since it gave me a lot of opportunity and i was able to become someone in my life. But this city has some very dark people whom we will never know when we are gonna meet them.

My guy friend and I were at Cubbon earlier this afternoon to meet up with some other friends. Two trans people came up to ask for money. obligingly took out my wallet to give them a 100 rupees. I am absolutely not earning enough for this to not be a problem. And it was cash given by my mother as blessing. Everyone I talk to is saying that I was the fool for letting it happen. But I was so in shock I couldn't do anything else.

raw_text: I was robbed in broad daylight.So it happened only 2 days ago on the 10th of April. I landed at the Bangalore Airport after flying in from a very hectic meeting at Ranchi. I booked an Ola cab and while I checked in the cab, I asked him to switch on the ac because it was very hot. He insisted that he won't be earning much money but apparently the booking amount was over 1200 INR. Later he goes in the petrol pump and reluctantly switches on the ac. After he reached me home from a very long route which shouldn't be the obvious one, I paid him through gpay on his PhonePe QR code. Somehow the payment doesn't go through and gets stuch in processing. He called me multiple times and I very kindly requested him to wait for the payment to go through. Next day he calls me again and again and had me pay him the entire amount of 1277 INR again. Some hours later i checked and saw that the earlier payment is also processed and has went through. Since then he has blocked me and is not taking any calls. I tried raising the payment dispute with OLA but apparently they have such a shitty AI chatbot working in place of real humans that it can't even understand my query and would not connect me to a human. Help me out here guys. Had it been some small amount like a few hundreds, it wouldn't matter much but this is a substantial amount of money. Any and all help will be appreciated.

I need advice to deal with my current situation and i hope to find it here

raw_text: I wanted to share my recent experience to warn others about a scam I encountered while I was travelling from mysore to bangalore in my Xcent. I’ve always been cautious, but this was a professional con job that caught me off guard. There was some space after a signal and a biker came in front of my car and told me to lower my window. I foolishly complied and he told me that sparks were visible inside the engine compartment. Just as I was opening the door to get out and see, the man came up, and asked me to open the bonnet quickly. I did so, and by the time I could unbuckle the seatbelt and get out, he had the bonnet open and there was smoke. I said that let me try to call the nearest dealership and asked for his phone (the signal on my cell was dead for a good while during this time - thanks Vodafone!!). He said they will take ages, but that he knows a mechanic nearby and we can ask for his help. It will take only 10 minutes...so I complied The mechanic came over and I let him open the bonnet while he told me to start the car. He had actually removed the spark plug from a cylinder and told me to come out and see the car jerking and the sparks being visible...me being so tensed, not knowing much about these things just kept nodding like a fool which he must have noticed to scam me more later... At that moment, I got scared and told him to fix what was necessary. He went under the car, did something I don’t know about because he told me to be seated inside the car and continue pressing the clutch till he signals me to stop. Once his work was done, he insisted on a test drive. My car was perfectly fine before this incident, but after his so-called repairs, it felt worse, and I suspected he might’ve swapped some parts for inferior ones. I told him about it to which he told this is how the car is supposed to actually feel while driving...didn't know what to say and just kept silent... Once the work was done, he informed me that he’d replaced the AC compressor and alternator, demanding 9000 rs for that along with another 1500 rs for service charge! I didn't have enough cash with me so had to drive to the nearest ATM and then handed over the amount and continued my journey (he denied gpay). As I headed to the city, I drove in to a dealership and described the incident as I was very uncomfortable with the driving experience after the mechanic fixed my car...kept feeling something was off They thoroughly checked my vehicle and told that the alternator and battery had been replaced but with old parts and it was a tactic from that person to rob the money and car parts and make me a fool...and since I don't understand Kannada, I became an easy target for them...I felt so angry at myself and still am as I type this... The final take from this expensive lesson is never to open the bonnet unless you are there. I had this rule for the dicky, because I was told that even security personnel were not above planting stuff in the dicky while checking and then claiming that they found contraband in the car...now this is another big lesson for me...very frustrated I got conned like this...

I said that let me try to call the nearest dealership and asked for his phone (the signal on my cell was dead for a good while during this time - thanks Vodafone!!). He said they will take ages, but that he knows a mechanic nearby and we can ask for his help. It will take only 10 minutes...so I complied The mechanic came over and I let him open the bonnet while he told me to start the car. He had actually removed the spark plug from a cylinder and told me to come out and see the car jerking and the sparks being visible...me being so tensed, not knowing much about these things just kept nodding like a fool which he must have noticed to scam me more later... At that moment, I got scared and told him to fix what was necessary. He went under the car, did something I don’t know about because he told me to be seated inside the car and continue pressing the clutch till he signals me to stop. Once his work was done, he insisted on a test drive. My car was perfectly fine before this incident, but after his so-called repairs, it felt worse, and I suspected he might’ve swapped some parts for inferior ones. I told him about it to which he told this is how the car is supposed to actually feel while driving...didn't know what to say and just kept silent... Once the work was done, he informed me that he’d replaced the AC compressor and alternator, demanding 9000 rs for that along with another 1500 rs for service charge! I didn't have enough cash with me so had to drive to the nearest ATM and then handed over the amount and continued my journey (he denied gpay). As I headed to the city, I drove in to a dealership and described the incident as I was very uncomfortable with the driving experience after the mechanic fixed my car...kept feeling something was off They thoroughly checked my vehicle and told that the alternator and battery had been replaced but with old parts and it was a tactic from that person to rob the money and car parts and make me a fool...and since I don't understand Kannada, I became an easy target for them...I felt so angry at myself and still am as I type this... The final take from this expensive lesson is never to open the bonnet unless you are there. I had this rule for the dicky, because I was told that even security personnel were not above planting stuff in the dicky while checking and then claiming that they found contraband in the car...now this is another big lesson for me...very frustrated I got conned like this...

raw_text: Brutal attack on my brother by men with a machete in hebbal My brother was attacked by two individuals with a machete at the shell petrol pump near hebbal yesterday. They were drunk and riding scooter. The incident transpired when my brother honked at them for driving erratically. They approached him at the petrol pump trying to grab the keys of his bike and my brother tried to push them to stop them from it. One of the men then proceeded to take out a machete and slashed at my brother 4-5 times from behind. He was hit on his bag and his arm. He was hit brutally and is extremely lucky to have not been greviously injured. As is the usual with police we do not have enough faith that they will hold the accused accountable to the maximum degree They have pressed charges of criminal intimidation and assault with a weapon But this was clearly an attack on his life. Attempted murder. he was hit from behind on his back. When he turned around he was slashed on his arm. Entire incident is on the Cctv of the shell petrol pump I don't know how to proceed further in this matter. My brother is speaking to a lawyer. Should we also contact the press? And how to approach the media in this case? Edit: This incident happened in a crowded area in the evening at 5.30-6 pm so it was not even late at night as some people in the comments are thinking. He was not robbed. He ran for cover after the initial blows. One of the goons tried to chase him initially but then they ran away. It is really horrifying that it happened out in the open in a busy area like this. To me It just goes to show the extent of lawlessness, these goons clearly have no fear of the police. Thank you for your kind words everyone. Brother is stitched up but fine now.

raw_text: So me and my friend , we went on to have chai on Sarjapur road at a cafe called Gabbar chai. We had gone to that place one or two times before , but this particular time we parked the car just beside the cafe. Our bags were in backseat and it was 6:30 pm in evening so not that dark. We went in and ordered chai and before chai came , it wasn't even 10 minutes and waiter came nonchalantly and told us that someone hit our car. When we went outside , we saw that someone broke back window of car and took both of our bags which had laptops and wallets . We wrote FIR the very next day and police even came to spot check with us. But there's still no significant improvement At the time of incident, when police came they asked the waiters why they haven't installed camera after last incident and then we found out apparently 2-3 similar incident had occurred in same place in that week . This happened on 12th april. So, somehow it feels that they were in on it. Luckily, our laptops were company laptops and they were insured and we blocked our cards that were in wallet. Cash is definitely gone now. I wanted to ask if this has become common in bangalore now and what measures i should do to prevent this apart from not keeping any valuables in car.

raw_text: I'm a student studying Btech 1st year, yesterday someone stolen my mobile when I was getting into a bus. I was in a bad mood I wasn't mentally active, standing at the stop was thinking about something else suddenly bus came everyone rushed. Two guys blocked me entering door, I tried to sneak through them to get a seat in a moment I felt something missed I checked my pocket it was empty. My reflexes are too bad I couldn't even shoutout I turned back checked everyone all were students. I got off the bus someone pointed towards opposite road but no one was there. I suspected a wrong guy and it was too late, I couldn't do anything much tried to locate on find my device got no luck. I left the spot in vain. I can't express the feeling after that lost mobile isn't making me feel sad but that feel of irresponsible and helplessness making me sad to the gut. This is my story. The thing is I hear everyday someone lost their mobile,wallet in BMTC buses from my college, friend's college. Can't we do anything? Isn't there anyway to stop all this? I complained and filed FIR online I know it's useless other than this is there any other way to stop this nuisance? Irony is there was a lady police but she didn't even look at my side when i was panicked and searching everyone. My question is we live in so called 'Silicon valley of India' can't anyone find a solution,any device or an application to end this? I was irresponsible this time i'm sure not everyone will be like this. No one can stay 100% active each time. I'm new to this travelling through BMTC buses but learnt a lesson. Please share any safer way to hold mobile so that next time I shouldn't fall for this. Location: Yelahanka Old town opposite to hanuman temple & NES bus stop

raw_text: Got beat up by a local pretending to be police by accusing posession of marijuana.I want to share a shocking incident that took place on the night of the IPL Final. A group of four friends, including myself, were peacefully hanging out at my home in *** Layout when an unexpected and alarming incident occurred. Out of nowhere, a man, approximately aged 28-30, rang our doorbell and forcefully entered our premises. He immediately punched me in the face, which was completely unprovoked. Unfortunately, one of my friends happened to have a small quantity of marijuana with him at that time. The situation quickly escalated as the assailant continued to physically assault both me and my friend, while impersonating a police officer. He threatened us, claiming that a police vehicle was on its way and warned that he would inflict further harm if we dared to involve the actual police. Furthermore, he filmed a video of us, clearly displaying the marijuana he had taken from us as evidence. Forcing us outside onto the road, the assailant ordered us to wait for the arrival of the police. We waited for an extended period until, unexpectedly, he returned the confiscated marijuana and instructed me and my two friends to go back inside the house. It was then that he attempted to negotiate with my other two friends, demanding an excessive amount of money. However, when they explained that they didn't have the requested sum, he abruptly left the scene. Throughout the ordeal, the assailant also showed us various videos and images he had collected from similar situations. This has left me wondering who these individuals are and why they seem to operate outside the bounds of the law. I managed to obtain his vehicle number, which could potentially aid in identifying him and bringing him to justice. I am reaching out to the Reddit community for help. If anyone has any information or advice on how to proceed with this incident, it would be greatly appreciated. Together, let's ensure that those who take the law into their own hands are held accountable for their actions.

raw_text: I [M 26], was with my two sisters both older than me, one has a 2.5 year old baby, my mother who has severe arthritis and walks with a stick was also with us. Jaynangar 5th block, near Sri Kumaran Silk, we were shopping sarees. We booked and uber, it came, I took the front seat my sisters and mom sat at the back. As my sister was getting in, an auto came and very lightly knicked the car's door, so light I didn't even realise sitting in front seat. My sister saw the auto and since it was nothing she continued to get into the car, as she shut the door the auto stopped next to us, the driver came, leaned against my sister's window and started yelling at my sister, my cab driver had just started driving but he stopped and talked to the auto driver. Since I know the hooliganism of auto drivers here I aksed my driver to just drive but he sat still like he did not have ears, I asked him what the auto driver is saying, and he said nothing just looked at me, the driver kept yelling at my sister. Some people form Kumaran silk came down(since we came out of their shop) and started talking to the angry auto driver. About 1-2 mins of trying to get my driver to drive away and failing, I got out of the car to sort it. By then it was about 20 people around the car all shouting in kannada. I thought the rest were there to help but I was mistaken. After they had had their discussion, they all collectively started to convince me to give money, without explaining anything. I refused and sat back in the car, one guy came to my window and started asking me for money and didn't let me shut the window. I asked my driver to go and he sat like he's not even there, all this while the auto driver is screaming at us, from driver's window because he kept it open, road head was not blocked. This happened at about 4-5 pm in Jaynagar 5th Block in busy traffic. Ultimately they agreed on 200 rupees, which I wouldn't have given if I was alone, but with 3 women and a kid, I had no option, as I gave him the money he stepped in his auto drove away while shouting at me. Then finally my driver decided to start the car and go.
